questions.question: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. Children of such courage and good sense (1)/ must be well equipped to serve they're country (2)/ when they grow up. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The possessive pronoun 'their' should be used in place of the contraction "they're" (they are). A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select 'No Error'. The three wanderers turned (1)/ into a side street and stepped into (2)/ a little shop where food was sold. (3)/ No error (4)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct and free of error. D is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Find the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is Bellwether.

questions.question: In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Find the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is Fahrenheit.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the Idiom/Phrase underlined in the sentence. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. After the new rules and regulations have come into effect, the ball is in your court.
options: It is up to you to make the next decision or step
You should be more active and sportive
You should listen to others before deciding anything
You should abide by all the rules and regulations
questions.explanation: "The ball is in your court" means "It is up to you to make the next decision or step"

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences. Attempting to avoid notice or attention
questions.explanation: Imposter: a person who pretends to be someone else in order to deceive others. Furtive: attempting to avoid notice or attention, typically because of guilt or a belief that discovery would lead to trouble; secretive. Dishonest: a person who cannot be trusted and likely to steal, cheat, or lie. Corrupt: a person who dishonestly uses his position or power to get an advantage, especially for money.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with an appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. Please see that you ____ do any damage
questions.explanation: It is wrong to use the future tense in the subordinate clause when the verb in the main clause is in the imperative mood. Use of "Have not" will make the sentence grammatically incorrect. Hence, 'do not' is the correct answer choice.

questions.question: Four alternatives are given for the Idiom/Phrase underlined in the sentence. Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase and mark it. If you don't plan your preparation for this exam, you'll only be chasing your tail.
options: Doing a lot but achieving little
Trying to be clever to achieve something
Do as expected or as promised
questions.explanation: 'Chasing your tail' means doing a lot but achieving little.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentences and mark it. A feeling of fear or anxiety about something that may happen
questions.explanation: 'Epiphany' is a moment of sudden revelation or clarity. 'Misogyny' is defined as the dislike of or the prejudice ingrained against women. 'Ablution' is the act of washing oneself. The word for a feeling of fear or anxiety about an event that may happen in the future is 'trepidation'. Another word for this is 'foreboding'.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence is given with a blank to be filled in with an appropriate word. Four alternatives are suggested for each question. Choose the correct alternative out of the four. His mother was unusually _______ that afternoon.
questions.explanation: The word 'quite' means 'fairly'. 'Quit' means to give up. A 'quilt' is a duvet. The word that fits in the context is 'quiet' which means 'silent'.

questions.question: The following question contains a word in bold followed by four words. Select the word that is most similar in meaning to the word in bold. Massacre
questions.explanation: Massacre is mass killing or slaughter.

questions.question: The following question contains a word in bold followed by four words. Select the word that is opposite in meaning to the word in bold. Luscious
questions.explanation: Luscious means having a pleasant sweet taste.

questions.question: The following question contains a word in bold, followed by four words. Select the word that is opposite in meaning to the word in bold. Vanquish
questions.explanation: 'Vanquish' means to defeat the opponent, while 'surrender' means to give up.

questions.question: Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. ADJURE
questions.explanation: Adjure means to command or urge. Example: She adjured him to tell the truth. Renounce- reject; abandon

questions.question: A part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose "No Improvement" option. America behaved as though (it was the) world police.
questions.explanation: Whenever we have a conditional clause or a hypothetical situation, the singular noun takes "were" as the supporting verb. The same rule is applicable to 'as if' also. Ex. I wish I were Batman.

questions.question: A part of the sentence is bracketed. Below are given alternatives to the bracketed part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative. In case no improvement is required, choose "No Improvement" option. (Two loads of bricks) arrived at our construction site yesterday.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is 'Two loads of brick arrived at our construction site yesterday'. Brick, stone etc. are uncountable and considered plural. So 'bricks' is a wrong usage.

questions.question: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active voice. Who has torn my currency notes?
options: My currency notes got teared down by whom?
By whom have my currency notes been torned?
By whom have my currency notes been teared?
By whom have my currency notes been torn?
questions.explanation: The given sentence is in active voice. In the passive voice, 'who' (subjective pronoun) should be replaced by by 'whom' (objective pronoun). Also, the verb should be present in its past participle form. The correct past participle form of the verb 'tear' is 'torn'. So, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: great poets is a ______.
questions.explanation: Voyage: A long journey involving travel by sea. Career: An occupation undertaken for a significant period Course: The route or direction of a ship, aircraft, road or river Rhythm: A systematic arrangement of musical sounds. Here, the best suited word is 'rhythm' as it is the only word related to poets. Additionally, the sentence after the blank mentions the context of 'melody', which again indicates that 'rhythm' will be the appropriate fit.

questions.question: therefore, is a ______ life.
questions.explanation: The article 'a' precedes the blank, indicating that option (b) and (d) can be eliminated. 'Impecunious' means having little or no money. 'Insatiable' means impossible to satisfy.' Option (c) is non-contextual as life without vision cannot be a 'matured' life. Mutilated' means inflicted with serious damage or spoiled. Life without vision is a mutilated life means life without vision is a spoiled life. So, the suitable filler is 'mutilated'.

questions.question: he seeks to ______ for something
questions.explanation: 'Despair' means the complete loss or absence of hope. 'Persuade' means to induce someone to do something. 'Respire' means to breathe. 'Aspire' means to wish for something. Here the most suited word is 'aspire' as none of the other words fir the context.

questions.question: the ideal of ______ which can
questions.explanation: 'Deception' means make someone believe something which is not true. 'Introspection' means the act of looking with in oneself. 'Circumspect' means careful. 'Suspicion' means a feeling that something will happen. Option (a) and (c) are eliminated as the tone of the sentence is not negative. Option (b) is eliminated as it is non-contextual. Here, the suitable word is 'introspection'.

questions.question: to be ______ through vision
questions.explanation: Intrigued: to arouse the curiosity or interest interpolate: to put something between other things Interpret: understand the meaning of something Introspect: to look into oneself So the suitable word is 'interpreted'.

questions.question: Change the form from Direct to Indirect Speech or vice-versa He said, "I will never beat her up again."
options: He said that he would never beat her up again.
He said to never to beat her up again.
He said that he will never beat her up again.
questions.explanation: When converting from direct to indirect speech, the simple future tense (will) changes into the future conditional (would). The answer is a.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences, and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. There was a muddled maze of dusty furniture - the breakfast- room furniture from the old home where they had lived all their lives. Q. It looked much darker than the kitchen when they went in with the one candle. R. This was because the kitchen was whitewashed, but the dining-room was dark wood from floor to ceiling. S. The dining-room opened out of the kitchen.
questions.explanation: The passage begins with S, as this sentence lays the foundation of the subject to be discussed in the passage. Q tells us how the two rooms looked in comparison to each other. R tells us the reason why they looked so. P concludes by telling us about the furniture in the rooms. The order is SQRP.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, four sentences are given, which when properly arranged, make up a paragraph. Rearrange the given sentences, and choose the option that provides the correct final order. P. When you are in the train, it seems such a little way between post and post. Q. They walked along the edge of the line, and heard the telegraph wires humming over their heads. R. One after another the posts seem to catch up the wires almost more quickly than you can count them. S. But when you have to walk, the posts seem few and far between.
questions.explanation: The passage begins laying the foundation of the topic to be discussed in the passage, which is done by Q. P and R are a pair: P talks about how the distance between two posts seem very small in a train, and R follows by telling us further about it. S concludes by telling us how this is different from when one has to walk. The order is QPRS.
